note: ERNIE native canvas is 1024²; multi-layer variants tile horizontally at runtime. Target resolutions (per INT-18 spec): NES-era 3200×224 single-layer · SNES/Genesis 3-layer 3200×224-per-layer · Mode-7 1024² tileable source.

parallax_single.note: NES-era single-scroll-register hardware. One image, scrolled at foreground rate (or slowed ratio, scene-defined).

parallax_3layer.note: SNES/Genesis/arcade era. Same biome + time_of_day across layers; detail + scroll-ratio differ. SEED SHARING: pin the same seed across far/mid/near calls → preserves biome-identity across layers.

layers.name: mode7_horizon
layers.note: static upper band — sky + distant horizon

layers.name: mode7_floor
layers.note: tileable 1024² source; runtime does rotation + perspective warp

mode7.note: SMK / F-Zero / shmup-over-planet. The floor tile is the expensive output; horizon is static overlay.

skybox_static.note: Non-scrolling backdrop. Used by boss-arena-lock scenes or fixed screens (JRPG battle-screen backdrop).

output_strategy.mode7: one 1024² tileable PNG + one 3200×112 horizon strip

implementation_notes: Workflow is sister-spec'd (INT-18) → main-implemented 2026-04-22 after Shoal went dead. Same shape as tileable_terrain (ERNIE doesn't know about tileability — postprocess handles seamlessness).
Canary-phase ships 3 backdrops proving the 3 axes: single (Raiden vertical military), 3-layer (Sonic green-hill-zone), mode7 (Super Mario Kart track-side).
Future sub-workflow `parallax_animated` could replace static fog/aurora/waterfall layers with short N-frame cycles. Not in v1.
